suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'blur', 'data', 'opacity', 'radius', and 'standard'. Either include them or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [map, zIndex, onLoadStart, onLoadComplete, onError, data, standard, opacity, radius, blur]

fix.text: [map, zIndex, onLoadStart, onLoadComplete, onError, data, standard, opacity, radius, blur]

suppressedMessages.message: The ref value 'mapInstanceRef.current' will likely have changed by the time this effect cleanup function runs. If this ref points to a node rendered by React, copy 'mapInstanceRef.current' to a variable inside the effect, and use that variable in the cleanup function.

The ref value 'mapRef.current' will likely have changed by the time this effect cleanup function runs. If this ref points to a node rendered by React, copy 'mapRef.current' to a variable inside the effect, and use that variable in the cleanup function.

The ref value 'tileLayersRef.current' will likely have changed by the time this effect cleanup function runs. If this ref points to a node rendered by React, copy 'tileLayersRef.current' to a variable inside the effect, and use that variable in the cleanup function.

React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'blur', 'data', 'maxTemperature', 'minTemperature', 'onError', 'onLoadComplete', 'onLoadStart', 'opacity', and 'radius'. Either include them or remove the dependency array. If 'onLoadStart' changes too often, find the parent component that defines it and wrap that definition in useCallback.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [map, useWeatherTiles, apiKey, tileLayer, zIndex, onLoadStart, opacity, onError, onLoadComplete, data, radius, blur, maxTemperature, minTemperature]

fix.text: [map, useWeatherTiles, apiKey, tileLayer, zIndex, onLoadStart, opacity, onError, onLoadComplete, data, radius, blur, maxTemperature, minTemperature]

suppressedMessages.message: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect was passed a dependency list that is not an array literal. This means we can't statically verify whether you've passed the correct dependencies.
React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'execute' and 'immediate'. Either include them or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [execute, immediate]

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook React.useEffect has a missing dependency: 'options'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [prefetchQuery, queryKey, queryFn, options.staleTime, options.cacheTime, options.prefetch, options]

fix.text: [prefetchQuery, queryKey, queryFn, options.staleTime, options.cacheTime, options.prefetch, options]

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook React.useEffect has an unnecessary dependency: 'queryClient'. Either exclude it or remove the dependency array. Outer scope values like 'queryClient' aren't valid dependencies because mutating them doesn't re-render the component.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [queryKey, options.staleTime, options.backgroundRefresh]
